column2: I'm in Year 12 in Queensland right now. What's the minimum ATAR I need to get into the BIT at UQ?

column3: Your QTAC preference order can affect which offers you receive, so you should think carefully about how you rank your program preferences. For personalised advice on how to order your preferences, contact QTAC directly at qtac.edu.au.

column2: Does UQ consider special circumstances or misadventure when assessing my BIT application?
column3: Yes. QTAC has provisions for special consideration if your Year 12 results were affected by illness, misadventure, or other circumstances. You should apply for this through QTAC; details are available at qtac.edu.au.

column2: I completed the International Baccalaureate. Does that qualify me to apply for the BIT as a domestic student?
column3: Yes. The International Baccalaureate is an accepted qualification for domestic BIT applications and will be converted to an equivalent entry score by QTAC. Ensure your subjects meet the BIT prerequisite requirements, and check study.uq.edu.au for details.

column2: What is the QTAC code for the BE(Hons)/IT dual degree?
column3: The QTAC code for the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) / Information Technology is not confirmed in the documents I have access to. Please check qtac.edu.au or study.uq.edu.au for the correct code.

column2: I'm in Year 12 in Queensland. What subjects do I need to have studied to get into the BIT?
column3: You need to meet the mathematics prerequisite for the BIT. Check the specific subject requirements at study.uq.edu.au, as prerequisites are reviewed annually and it is important to confirm the current requirements before applying.

column2: What is the QTAC code I need to use when applying for the Bachelor of Information Technology?
column3: The QTAC code for the Bachelor of Information Technology at UQ is 733001. You apply through QTAC at qtac.edu.au.

column2: I missed the minimum entry score for BIT this year. Can I still get in somehow?
column3: There may be alternative pathways such as UQ's adjustment factors or alternative entry schemes that could affect your eligibility. Contact UQ Admissions or QTAC directly to explore your options, as meeting the minimum does not guarantee admission and pathways vary.

column2: What was the median entry score for the BIT for Semester 1, 2026?
column3: The median entry score for the Bachelor of Information Technology for Semester 1, 2026 was 87.4, with a minimum of 81.9 and a highest of 95.45. Entry scores are reviewed annually, so check study.uq.edu.au for the most current figures.

column2: What's the minimum entry score for the BCom/IT dual degree?
column3: The minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Commerce / Information Technology is 84.4 for Semester 1, 2026. Check study.uq.edu.au for updated figures, as entry scores are reviewed annually.

column2: How does my school's ranking or ranking adjustments affect my BIT application through QTAC?
column3: QTAC may apply adjustment factors based on your school or subjects, which can affect your final entry score. Contact QTAC directly at qtac.edu.au for details on which adjustments apply to your situation.

column2: I finished Year 12 a couple of years ago and didn't apply for uni straight away. Can I still apply for the BIT as a domestic student?
column3: Yes, you can still apply through QTAC as a non-school leaver. Your application may be assessed on different criteria, and you should contact QTAC or UQ Admissions to confirm what supporting information is required.

column2: Does the BIT have a Semester 2 intake for domestic students?
column3: You should check study.uq.edu.au for the current intake availability for domestic students, as intake options can vary by program and year.

column2: What is the minimum entry score for the BE(Hons)/IT dual degree?
column3: The minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) / Information Technology is 84 for Semester 1, 2026. Entry scores are reviewed annually, so confirm current details at study.uq.edu.au.

column2: If I'm a domestic student who has done a TAFE qualification, can I use that to apply for the BIT instead of an ATAR?
column3: TAFE and VET qualifications can be considered for entry into UQ programs. You should apply through QTAC and contact UQ Admissions to confirm how your qualification will be assessed for the BIT.

column2: I live in NSW. Do I apply through QTAC or UAC for the BIT at UQ?
column3: Even as an interstate student, you apply for the BIT at UQ through QTAC, not UAC. Your NSW results will be converted to an equivalent entry score by QTAC.

column2: What happens if two applicants have the same entry score — how does UQ decide who gets in?
column3: Specific tie-breaking procedures are managed by QTAC. Contact QTAC at qtac.edu.au for details on how offers are made when applicants have identical entry scores.

column2: Is there a minimum English requirement for domestic students applying to the BIT?
column3: Domestic students who have completed Year 12 in Australia generally satisfy English requirements through their school results. If you have concerns about your English proficiency, contact UQ Admissions for clarification.

column2: I got an entry score above the minimum for BIT but I'm worried I won't get an offer. Is the minimum score a guarantee?
column3: No, meeting the minimum entry score of 81.9 does not guarantee admission, as offers depend on the number of available places and the scores of other applicants in that round. Check study.uq.edu.au for the latest entry score information.

column2: Can I apply for the BIT and the BBusMgt/IT dual degree at the same time through QTAC?
column3: Yes, QTAC allows you to list multiple program preferences in your application, so you can include both the BIT (QTAC code 733001) and the BBusMgt/IT (QTAC code 710401) as preferences. Rankings are used to allocate offers based on available places.

column2: What if I don't have the required maths subject from high school — are there any bridging options before starting BIT?
column3: UQ and external providers may offer bridging or preparation courses to help you meet prerequisite requirements. Contact UQ Admissions or check study.uq.edu.au for current bridging options available to you.

column2: I completed my Year 12 overseas but I'm a permanent resident. Do I apply as domestic or international for the BIT?
column3: As a permanent resident, you are generally considered a domestic student for fee purposes and apply through QTAC. Your overseas Year 12 results will be assessed for equivalency; contact QTAC or UQ Admissions to confirm how your qualifications are evaluated.

column2: When does Semester 1, 2026 start at UQ for new BIT students?
column3: Semester 1, 2026 commences on 23 February 2026.

column2: Are entry scores for the BIT the same every year, or do they change?
column3: Entry scores change annually and are reviewed each April. The figures published for Semester 1, 2026 — minimum 81.9, median 87.4, highest 95.45 — reflect that intake only. Always check study.uq.edu.au for the most current entry score information.

column2: I'm in Year 12 in Queensland right now. What's the minimum entry score I need to get into the BIT at UQ?
column3: The minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Information Technology (Semester 1, 2026) was 81.9, with a median of 87.4 and a highest of 95.45. Meeting the minimum does not guarantee admission, and entry scores are reviewed annually in April, so check study.uq.edu.au for the latest figures.

column2: Do I need to study any specific subjects in Year 12 to be eligible for the BIT?
column3: Yes, the BIT has prerequisite subject requirements. You should check study.uq.edu.au or contact UQ Admissions directly to confirm which subjects are required, as prerequisites can vary and are reviewed annually.

column2: What is the QTAC code I need to use when applying for the standalone Bachelor of Information Technology?
column3: The QTAC code for the Bachelor of Information Technology is 733001. Domestic and interstate students apply through QTAC at qtac.edu.au.

column2: I go to a school in regional Queensland — can I get any kind of entry score adjustment to help my application for BIT?
column3: QTAC administers adjustment schemes such as the Regional and Remote Adjustment that may apply to eligible applicants. You should check qtac.edu.au for the full list of available adjustments and eligibility criteria, as UQ cannot guarantee specific adjustment amounts.

column2: What's the minimum entry score for the BBusMgt/IT dual degree if I want to apply as a domestic student?
column3: The minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Business Management / Information Technology dual degree is 86.4 for Semester 1, 2026. Entry scores are reviewed annually, so confirm the latest figures at study.uq.edu.au.

column2: I didn't finish Year 12 but I've been working in IT for a few years. Can I still apply for the BIT as a domestic student?
column3: UQ may consider non-Year-12 applicants through alternative entry pathways. You should contact UQ Admissions or visit study.uq.edu.au to explore whether your work experience or other qualifications meet entry requirements.

column2: If I do a diploma at TAFE first, can I use that to get into the BIT instead of relying on my ATAR?
column3: Completing a relevant TAFE diploma may qualify you for alternative entry into the BIT. You should contact UQ Admissions or check study.uq.edu.au to confirm which TAFE qualifications are recognised and whether any credit transfer applies.

column2: What's the minimum entry score I'd need for the BCom/IT dual degree as a domestic applicant?
column3: The minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Commerce / Information Technology dual degree is 84.4 for Semester 1, 2026. Check study.uq.edu.au for the most current figures, as scores are reviewed annually.

column2: I'm thinking about the BE(Hons)/IT — is the entry score higher than for the standard BIT?
column3: Yes, the minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) / IT is 84 for Semester 1, 2026, which is higher than the 81.9 minimum for the standalone BIT. Both scores are reviewed annually, so verify the latest at study.uq.edu.au.

column2: When does Semester 1, 2026 actually start if I get accepted into the BIT?
column3: Semester 1, 2026 commences on 23 February 2026. Confirm key dates including orientation and enrolment deadlines through the UQ academic calendar at study.uq.edu.au.

column2: I'm from Vietnam and just finished high school. What qualification do I need to apply for the BIT at UQ?
column3: UQ accepts a range of international Year 12 equivalents for BIT entry. You should check study.uq.edu.au to confirm whether your specific Vietnamese qualification is recognised and what the equivalent entry score requirement is.

column2: What is the minimum entry score for international students applying to the BIT for Semester 1, 2026?
column3: The minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Information Technology for Semester 1, 2026 was 81.9, with a median of 87.4 and a highest of 95.45. Meeting the minimum does not guarantee admission, and entry scores are reviewed annually.

column2: I'm an international student. Do I need to show proof of funds to get a student visa for Australia?
column3: Yes. When applying for an Australian student visa, you must demonstrate evidence of living costs of at least AUD $29,710 as part of the visa requirements.

column2: If I get a student visa, how many hours can I actually work during semester?
column3: From 1 July 2023, student visa holders can work up to 48 hours per fortnight during semester. Always confirm current visa conditions with the Department of Home Affairs, as rules can change.

column2: I studied the IB (International Baccalaureate). Is that accepted for entry into the BIT?
column3: UQ accepts the International Baccalaureate as a Year 12 equivalent for entry. You should confirm your IB score meets the entry requirements and that any prerequisites are satisfied by checking study.uq.edu.au or contacting UQ Admissions.

column2: What English proficiency test scores does UQ accept for international BIT applicants?
column3: UQ accepts several English proficiency tests including IELTS, TOEFL, and PTE Academic. Specific minimum score requirements vary, so check study.uq.edu.au for the current English language requirements for the BIT.

column2: I completed my schooling in China using the Gaokao. Will UQ accept that for BIT entry?
column3: UQ does consider international qualifications including the Chinese Gaokao for entry. You should check study.uq.edu.au or contact UQ Admissions to confirm the specific score requirements and whether your English proficiency requirement can be satisfied.

column2: Is there a non-refundable fee I have to pay when I submit my international application to UQ?
column3: Yes. A non-refundable application fee of AUD $150 applies to all international applications, regardless of how the application is submitted.

column3: UQ offers pathway programs for students who do not yet meet direct entry English requirements. You should visit study.uq.edu.au or contact UQ Admissions to find out which pathway options are available for your situation.

column2: I'm applying as an international student — can I apply directly to UQ or do I have to go through an agent?

column2: Does the BIT have a mathematics prerequisite, and does that apply to international students too?
column3: Yes, the BIT has a mathematics prerequisite that applies to both domestic and international applicants. You should confirm the specific mathematics subject requirements for your qualification at study.uq.edu.au.

column2: What is the minimum entry score for international students applying to the BE(Hons)/IT dual degree?
column3: The minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) / Information Technology is 84. Entry scores are reviewed annually, so check study.uq.edu.au for the most current information.

column2: I'm applying from overseas — when does Semester 2, 2026 start if I miss the Semester 1 intake?

column2: I finished Year 12 in New Zealand. Can I still apply for the BIT at UQ as an international student?

column3: A grade of 65 per cent or better in English from the CBSE satisfies UQ's Minimum English requirement for the BIT. You should also ensure your overall results meet the entry score requirement and that the mathematics prerequisite is met.

column2: I'm an international student worried about costs. How much should I budget for living expenses if I live off campus in Brisbane?
column3: Off-campus living costs are approximately AUD $2,026–$4,151 per month. If you prefer on-campus residential colleges, costs are approximately AUD $2,635–$4,168 per month, with meals included.

column2: Can I apply for the BIT for a Semester 2 intake as an international student, or is it only available in Semester 1?
column3: The Bachelor of Information Technology is available for both Semester 1 and Semester 2 intakes for international students. Semester 2, 2026 commences on 27 July 2026.

column2: I'm an international student from India. What English proficiency score do I need to get into the BIT at UQ?

column2: What is the minimum entry score for the Bachelor of Information Technology for international students applying for Semester 1, 2026?
column3: The minimum entry score for the BIT (QTAC code 733001) for Semester 1, 2026 was 81.9, with a median of 87.4 and a highest of 95.45. Meeting the minimum does not guarantee admission, and entry scores are reviewed annually.

column3: UQ accepts a range of international qualifications, including the Chinese Gaokao, as part of its admissions process. You should check study.uq.edu.au or contact UQ Admissions directly to confirm how your Gaokao results will be assessed for BIT entry.

column2: How much money do I need to show as proof of living costs when applying for an Australian student visa to study at UQ?
column3: For a student visa application, you must demonstrate a minimum of AUD $29,710 to cover living costs. This is a Commonwealth requirement, so you should also check the Department of Home Affairs website for any updates.

column2: If I'm an international student, how many hours per week can I work while studying the BIT at UQ?
column3: From 1 July 2023, international students on a student visa are permitted to work up to 48 hours per fortnight during semester. Unlimited hours are generally allowed during scheduled course breaks.

column2: What is the non-refundable application fee I need to pay as an international student applying to UQ?
column3: All international applicants must pay a non-refundable application fee of AUD $150, regardless of how the application is submitted.

column2: I don't have an agent. Can I apply directly to UQ as an international student?
column3: Yes, you can apply directly to UQ as an international student without using an agent. If you prefer agent assistance, UQ works with approved agents worldwide — find one at study.uq.edu.au/contact/find-approved-uq-agent-near-you.

column2: When does Semester 1, 2026 start at UQ for international students?
column3: Semester 1, 2026 commences on 23 February 2026. You should plan your visa application and travel arrangements to ensure you arrive before this date.

column2: What are the living cost estimates I should budget for as an international student at UQ?
column3: Off-campus living costs are approximately AUD $2,026–$4,151 per month, while on-campus residential colleges cost approximately AUD $2,635–$4,168 per month with meals included. These figures are indicative, so budget carefully based on your lifestyle.

column2: I completed the International Baccalaureate (IB). Am I eligible to apply for the BIT as an international student?
column3: The International Baccalaureate is accepted as a qualifying qualification for entry to UQ. You should confirm that your IB results meet the BIT entry requirements and that you satisfy any subject prerequisites by checking study.uq.edu.au.

column2: What is the annual international tuition fee for the BIT in 2026?
column3: The 2026 first-year international tuition fee for the Bachelor of Information Technology is AUD $58,056 per year, with an indicative total program fee of approximately AUD $201,774 over three years. Fees are reviewed annually.

column2: Is there a Semester 2 intake for international students wanting to start the BIT?

column2: If I don't meet the English language requirement, are there pathway programs at UQ that can help me get into the BIT?
column3: UQ does offer pathway programs for students who do not yet meet direct entry requirements. Visit study.uq.edu.au for details on available pathway options and how they relate to BIT entry.

column2: I'm applying from Vietnam. Do I need to submit certified translations of my academic documents?
column3: UQ requires that all documents not in English be accompanied by certified English translations. Check study.uq.edu.au or contact UQ Admissions for the specific document requirements for Vietnamese qualifications.

column2: What is the SSAF and how much will I be charged as an international student at UQ in 2026?
column3: The Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) is a Commonwealth-mandated fee that funds non-academic student services. For 2026, the maximum annual SSAF is AUD $373.

column2: I completed my A-Levels in the UK. Will that be enough to apply for the BIT at UQ as an international student?
column3: UK A-Levels are generally accepted as a qualifying international qualification for entry to UQ. You should verify that your results meet the BIT minimum entry requirements and confirm any subject prerequisites at study.uq.edu.au.

column2: Are there any subject prerequisites I need to have studied in high school before I can apply for the BIT?

column2: Can I defer my offer for the BIT as an international student if I can't start in the intake I applied for?

column2: What is the minimum IELTS score needed for the Bachelor of Information Technology?

column2: I finished high school in China with the Gaokao. Will UQ accept that for BIT entry?

column2: If I don't meet the English proficiency requirement, is there any pathway I can take to still get into BIT?

column2: I completed a diploma at a TAFE in Australia. Can I use that to apply for BIT instead of relying on my overseas high school results?
